# Quantum Computing Reimagined for Real-World Impact ​
![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_Dark_bg_tex_left.webp)
![Infleqtion Sqale Quantum Computer](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Quantum_Computer_1000px.webp)
![Infleqtion Sqale Quantum Computer logo](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Sqale_RGB_White-1024x372.png)
## Infleqtion Quantum Computer
Sqale, Infleqtion’s line of full-stack , fault-tolerant neutral atom quantum computers, delivers scalability, speed, and fidelity—bringing quantum advantage closer than ever.
![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_Light_bg_tex_right.webp)
## Quantum Computing Unlocks the Classically Impossible
Quantum computing is a fundamentally new computing paradigm that uses qubits—obeying the laws of quantum mechanics—to harness quantum superposition and entanglement for computational power. These capabilities unlock a class of algorithms inaccessible to classical systems (CPUs, GPUs, TPUs).
Certain calculations that would take the world’s fastest supercomputer longer than Earth’s lifespan can theoretically be solved with a few hundred quantum logical qubits. This leap changes what’s possible in [materials science](https://infleqtion.com/materials-science/), [AI](https://infleqtion.com/ai-and-ml/), [energy optimization](https://infleqtion.com/energy-and-resource-exploration/), [life sciences](https://infleqtion.com/life-sciences-and-drug-discovery/), and more.
![Classical Bits versus Qubits in Quantum Computing](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Classical-v-Quantum-Bits-Infleqtion.png)
![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_White_bg.webp)
## Advanced Neutral Atom Quantum Computer
![neutral atom quantum computing diagram by Infleqtion using Sqale quantum computer](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Quantum_Computing_Tech_Drawing_transparent_v4-1024x376.png)
Infleqtion’s quantum devices are everywhere—even orbiting the globe on the International Space Station—and our Sqale quantum computers have been installed in national labs worldwide. Our architecture combines:
  * **Large demonstrated neutral atom arrays** —up to 1600 sites.
  * **Highest entangling gate fidelity** —99.73% ± 0.03%, a leader in the industry for neutral atoms.
  * **Individual optical addressing of atomic qubits** —unmatched in the industry.
  * **Deep integration of[Superstaq](https://infleqtion.com/quantum-software/)**, Infleqtion’s optimizing compiler with open-source frontends in Cirq and Qiskit.


![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_Dark_bg.webp)
## Our Path to Quantum Advantage
Infleqtion’s Sqale quantum computing roadmap is designed to move rapidly from today’s industry-leading neutral atom systems to utility-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computers. By integrating hardware, software, and error-correction advancements, we’re accelerating the arrival of commercially viable quantum computing.
[Download Roadmap](https://pub-e7f4da12b1c447829d3741851f5b79b4.r2.dev/Quantum-Computer-Roadmap-September-2025.pdf)
[Watch Our Recent Roadmap Webinar](https://youtu.be/75V0QtNbwzI)
![Infleqtion quantum computing roadmap 2025](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Infleqtion_Roadmap_onDark_2025.png)Roadmap updated September 2025
![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_Light_bg_tex_left.webp)
![16x16 Neutral Atom Array Infleqtion Quantum Computing](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/16x16array.png)
## Infleqtion Quantum Computing Achievements
  * **August 2024:** Infleqtion published a user-facing CZ fidelity of 99.73% ±0.03%, the highest entangling gate fidelity across the neutral atom quantum computing industry. ([APS PRX Quantum](https://journals.aps.org/prxquantum/abstract/10.1103/66s8-jj18))
  * **December 2024** : In a joint demonstration with NVIDIA, Infleqtion calculated the ground state of an Anderson Impurity Model system with logically encoded qubits – first in the world to apply quantum error detection to materials science. ([NVIDIA](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-cuda-q-runs-breakthrough-logical-qubit-application-on-infleqtion-qpu/))
  * **February 2025:** Largest neutral atom array in UK (16×16). [Read more.](https://info.infleqtion.com/news/infleqtion-demonstrates-largest-neutral-atom-array-in-uk-paving-way-for-scalable-quantum-computing)
  * **May 2025:** JP Morgan Collaboration—open-source quantum error correction library reducing physical qubit needs by 10–100×. [Read more.](https://infleqtion.com/infleqtion-unveils-open-source-library-for-quantum-error-correction-research/)
  * **September 2025:** Demonstration of a logical architecture uniting motion and in-place entanglement and world’s first execution of quantum decryption using logical qubits. [Read more.](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.13247)


![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_MediumDark_tex_right.webp)

## Built for Fault-Tolerant, Real-World Applications
  * Logical qubit operations at application level
  * Quantum error detection and non-destructive readout
  * Electric field compensation for stability
  * Individual addressed entangling gates
  * High shot rate and high gate fidelities
  * 2D-AOD beam steering for precision control


![Infleqtion Sqale Quantum Computer Rendering](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Table-Rendering-Sqale-Crop.png)
![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_MediumDark_bg.webp)
## Photonic-Integrated Circuits (PICs) for Scalable Neutral Atom Quantum Computing
Scaling quantum processor units (QPUs) to many thousands of qubits requires precise control of lasers for cooling, trapping, and Rydberg excitation. Today’s lab-scale systems rely on large laser systems on optical tables. Infleqtion is incorporating chip-scale photonics into our future quantum computing systems, enabling higher performance with massive C-SWaP reductions, through the use of PIC-based laser arrays, miniature frequency combs for laser frequency control, and fast MEMS based spatial light modulators for atom-addressing systems to scale computer operation. These PICs will reduce size, weight, and power by up to 10,000×, paving the way for desktop-class neutral atom quantum computers.
![Quantum Computing with integrated photonics](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/PICs_v2.png)
[Learn More About Integrated Photonics](https://infleqtion.com/integrated-photonics)

<h2 class="wp-block-heading" id="h-quantum-computing-unlocks-the-classically-impossible">Quantum Computing Unlocks the Classically Impossible</h2>



<p>Quantum computing is a fundamentally new computing paradigm that uses qubits—obeying the laws of quantum mechanics—to harness quantum superposition and entanglement for computational power. These capabilities unlock a class of algorithms inaccessible to classical systems (CPUs, GPUs, TPUs).</p>



<p>Certain calculations that would take the world’s fastest supercomputer longer than Earth’s lifespan can theoretically be solved with a few hundred quantum logical qubits. This leap changes what’s possible in <a href="https://infleqtion.com/materials-science/">materials science</a>, <a href="https://infleqtion.com/ai-and-ml/">AI</a>, <a href="https://infleqtion.com/energy-and-resource-exploration/">energy optimization</a>, <a href="https://infleqtion.com/life-sciences-and-drug-discovery/">life sciences</a>, and more.</p>
</div>

<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow" style="flex-basis:33.33%">
<p>Infleqtion’s quantum devices are everywhere—even orbiting the globe on the International Space Station—and our Sqale quantum computers have been installed in national labs worldwide. Our architecture combines:</p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>Large demonstrated neutral atom arrays</strong>—up to 1600 sites.</li>



<li><strong>Highest entangling gate fidelity</strong>—99.73% ± 0.03%, a leader in the industry for neutral atoms.</li>



<li><strong>Individual optical addressing of atomic qubits</strong>—unmatched in the industry.</li>



<li><strong>Deep integration of <a href="/quantum-software/">Superstaq</a></strong>, Infleqtion’s optimizing compiler with open-source frontends in Cirq and Qiskit.</li>
</ul>
</div>

<h2 class="wp-block-heading" id="h-infleqtion-quantum-computing-achievements">Infleqtion Quantum Computing Achievements</h2>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>August 2024: </strong>Infleqtion published a user-facing CZ fidelity of 99.73%  ±0.03%, the highest entangling gate fidelity across the neutral atom quantum computing industry. (<a href="https://journals.aps.org/prxquantum/abstract/10.1103/66s8-jj18">APS PRX Quantum</a>)</li>



<li><strong>December 2024</strong>: In a joint demonstration with NVIDIA, Infleqtion calculated the ground state of an Anderson Impurity Model system with logically encoded qubits – first in the world to apply quantum error detection to materials science. (<a href="https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-cuda-q-runs-breakthrough-logical-qubit-application-on-infleqtion-qpu/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">NVIDIA</a>)</li>



<li><strong>February 2025: </strong>Largest neutral atom array in UK (16×16). <a href="https://info.infleqtion.com/news/infleqtion-demonstrates-largest-neutral-atom-array-in-uk-paving-way-for-scalable-quantum-computing" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Read more.</a></li>



<li><strong>May 2025: </strong>JP Morgan Collaboration—open-source quantum error correction library reducing physical qubit needs by 10–100×. <a href="/infleqtion-unveils-open-source-library-for-quantum-error-correction-research/">Read more.</a></li>



<li><strong>September 2025: </strong>Demonstration of a logical architecture uniting motion and in-place entanglement and world’s first execution of quantum decryption using logical qubits. <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.13247" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Read more.</a></li>
</ul>
</div>

<h2 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-center" id="h-photonic-integrated-circuits-pics-for-scalable-neutral-atom-quantum-computing">Photonic-Integrated Circuits (PICs) for Scalable Neutral Atom Quantum Computing</h2>



<p class="has-text-align-center">Scaling quantum processor units (QPUs) to many thousands of qubits requires precise control of lasers for cooling, trapping, and Rydberg excitation. Today’s lab-scale systems rely on large laser systems on optical tables. Infleqtion is incorporating chip-scale photonics into our future quantum computing systems, enabling higher performance with massive C-SWaP reductions, through the use of PIC-based laser arrays, miniature frequency combs for laser frequency control, and fast MEMS based spatial light modulators for atom-addressing systems to scale computer operation. These PICs will reduce size, weight, and power by up to 10,000×, paving the way for desktop-class neutral atom quantum computers.</p>

        <script type="text/plain">
            (function () {
                function loadIframe(el) {
                    if (!el || el.dataset.loaded) return;
                    el.dataset.loaded = "1";
                    var id = el.getAttribute('data-ytid');
                    var src = 'https://www.youtube.com/embed/' + encodeURIComponent(id) + '?autoplay=1&rel=0';
                    var ifr = document.createElement('iframe');
                    ifr.setAttribute('src', src);
                    ifr.setAttribute('title', 'YouTube video');
                    ifr.setAttribute('frameborder', '0');
                    ifr.setAttribute('allow', 'accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share');
                    ifr.setAttribute('allowfullscreen', '1');
                    ifr.setAttribute('loading', 'lazy');
                    ifr.style.width = '100%';
                    ifr.style.height = '100%';
                    el.innerHTML = '';
                    el.appendChild(ifr);
                }

                // Click to play
                document.addEventListener('click', function (e) {
                    var el = e.target.closest('.yt-lite');
                    if (el) loadIframe(el);
                });

                // Keyboard accessible
                document.addEventListener('keydown', function (e) {
                    var active = document.activeElement;
                    if (!active || !active.classList || !active.classList.contains('yt-lite')) return;
                    if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') {
                        e.preventDefault();
                        loadIframe(active);
                    }
                });

                if ('IntersectionObserver' in window) {
                    var io = new IntersectionObserver(function (entries) {
                        entries.forEach(function (entry) {
                            if (entry.isIntersecting) entry.target.dataset.prepared = "1";
                        });
                    }, { rootMargin: '200px 0px' });
                    document.querySelectorAll('.yt-lite').forEach(function (el) { io.observe(el); });
                }
            })();
        </script>
        <style>
            /* Works with existing .video-grid/.video-item styles */
            .yt-lite {
                position: relative;
                cursor: pointer;
                background: #000;
                overflow: hidden;
                border-radius: 6px;
            }

            .yt-lite::before {
                content: "";
                display: block;
                padding-bottom: 56.25%;
            }

            /* 16:9 */
            .yt-lite>* {
                position: absolute;
                inset: 0;
                width: 100%;
                height: 100%;
            }

            .yt-lite .yt-thumb {
                width: 100%;
                height: 100%;
                object-fit: cover;
            }

            .yt-lite .yt-play {
                position: absolute;
                left: 50%;
                top: 50%;
                transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
                width: 68px;
                height: 48px;
                background: rgba(0, 0, 0, .6);
                border-radius: 12px;
                box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, .35);
            }

            .yt-lite .yt-play::before {
                content: "";
                position: absolute;
                left: 50%;
                top: 50%;
                transform: translate(-40%, -50%);
                border-style: solid;
                border-width: 12px 0 12px 20px;
                border-color: transparent transparent transparent #fff;
            }

            .yt-lite:focus {
                outline: 2px solid #6aa9ff;
                outline-offset: 2px;
            }

            .video-item p {
                text-align: center;
                margin-top: 12px;
                margin-bottom: 0;
            }
        </style>
